_comment: ShieldSync Labs - Azure 'deny expensive' guardrail (the SCP analog of infra/scp-sandbox-deny-expensive.json). ONE Custom Azure Policy that denies anything a free storage-exposure lab would never legitimately create: any VM above the tiny B1s tier, VM scale sets, AKS, any managed database (SQL / MySQL / PostgreSQL / Cosmos / MariaDB), public IPs, NAT gateways, Application Gateways, Load Balancers, plus a hard allowed-locations lock to eastus. Assign at the labs-subscription (or labs management-group) scope so it applies to EVERY leased RG. NOTE: Azure Policy rejects inline 'comment' keys inside policyRule conditions, so the per-condition notes live here instead: (1) REGION LOCK - deny anything outside allowedLocations (global resources report 'global' and pass); (2) VMs above the tiny burstable tier; (3) VM scale sets always denied; (4) managed Kubernetes; (5) managed databases; (6) costly networking; (7) other easy-to-forget spend (ACI, Redis, Synapse, HDInsight, Cognitive/OpenAI).
name: shieldsync-labs-deny-expensive

properties.displayName: ShieldSync Labs - deny expensive SKUs, DBs, networking; lock to eastus
properties.description: Cost + blast-radius guardrail for the ShieldSync Labs sandbox. Denies any resource that costs money at idle or enables an expensive escape (large VMs, VMSS, AKS, managed databases, public IPs, NAT gateways, App Gateways, Load Balancers) and pins all deployments to the eastus region. Storage accounts (the lab subject) are intentionally NOT denied.

metadata.description: Regions the labs sandbox may deploy into. Kept to eastus to match the deploysToRegion of the labs and keep teardown/RegionLock simple.
If a lab ever legitimately needs a VM, only these burstable micro sizes are permitted. Empty-by-intent for storage-only labs; kept as a parameter so the fence does not need editing later.
